title: helianthemum vulgare var. asperum
intro: Helianthemum vulgare var. asperum, also known as rough rockrose, is a variety of the common rockrose plant, which is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of rough rockrose include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herb in the treatment of various health conditions. Traditionally, rough rockrose has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
